messages.content: Create a systemd service for my Python app

```ini
# /etc/systemd/system/myapp.service
[Unit]
Description=My Python Application
After=network.target postgresql.service
Requires=postgresql.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=myapp
WorkingDirectory=/opt/myapp
EnvironmentFile=/opt/myapp/.env
ExecStart=/opt/myapp/venv/bin/uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000
Restart=always
RestartSec=5
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=myapp

# Security hardening
NoNewPrivileges=yes
PrivateTmp=yes
ProtectSystem=strict
ReadWritePaths=/opt/myapp/data

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable myapp
sudo systemctl start myapp
sudo systemctl status myapp
journalctl -u myapp -f  # follow logs
```
Set up a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for a Node.js app

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci-cd.yml
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  NODE_VERSION: '20'
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
          POSTGRES_DB: testdb
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
          cache: 'npm'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm test
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:testpass@localhost:5432/testdb

  build-and-push:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          push: true
          tags: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest

  deploy:
    needs: build-and-push
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to server
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.SERVER_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.SERVER_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.SSH_KEY }}
          script: |
            docker pull ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest
            docker-compose up -d
```
Monitor disk usage and alert if above 80%

<tool_call>{"name": "exec_command", "arguments": {"command": "df -h / /home /var | tail -n +2 | awk '{print $5, $6}'", "timeout": 5}}</tool_call>

```bash
# Script de monitoreo: /usr/local/bin/disk-monitor.sh
#!/bin/bash
THRESHOLD=80
df -h | tail -n +2 | while read line; do
    usage=$(echo $line | awk '{print $5}' | tr -d '%')
    mount=$(echo $line | awk '{print $6}')
    if [ "$usage" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        notify-send "⚠️ Disco lleno" "$mount al ${usage}% de uso"
        echo "ALERT: $mount at ${usage}%" | mail -s 'Disk Alert' admin@localhost
    fi
done
```

Programar cada 4 horas:
```bash
crontab -e
# Añadir:
0 */4 * * * /usr/local/bin/disk-monitor.sh
```
Configure Nginx as reverse proxy for my app
Clean up Docker to free space

<tool_call>{"name": "exec_command", "arguments": {"command": "docker system df", "timeout": 15}}</tool_call>

```bash
# Eliminar todo lo no usado
docker system prune -f          # contenedores parados, redes, imágenes dangling
docker volume prune -f          # volúmenes no usados
docker image prune -a -f        # TODAS las imágenes sin contenedor activo

# Ver espacio recuperado
docker system df
```

⚠️ `prune -a` elimina imágenes que no tienen contenedores corriendo, incluidas las cacheadas. Solo si tienes acceso a las imágenes en registry.
Show me the last 100 lines of my app's logs

<tool_call>{"name": "exec_command", "arguments": {"command": "journalctl -u myapp.service -n 100 --no-pager", "timeout": 10}}</tool_call>

Alternativamente si usa logs de archivo:
<tool_call>{"name": "exec_command", "arguments": {"command": "tail -100 /var/log/myapp/app.log", "timeout": 5}}</tool_call>
Setup automatic SSL certificate renewal with Let's Encrypt

```bash
# Instalar certbot
sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx

# Obtener certificado
sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com

# Verificar renovación automática (certbot instala timer systemd automáticamente)
systemctl status certbot.timer
certbot renew --dry-run  # test

# Si necesitas cron manual:
crontab -e
# Añadir:
0 3 * * * certbot renew --quiet && systemctl reload nginx
```

Certbot renueva automáticamente cuando faltan <30 días para expirar.
Create a bash script to backup and rotate logs

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# /usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh

LOG_DIR="/var/log/myapp"
BACKUP_DIR="/var/log/myapp/archive"
MAX_AGE_DAYS=30
MAX_SIZE_MB=100

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Comprimir logs del día anterior
for log_file in "$LOG_DIR"/*.log; do
    [ -f "$log_file" ] || continue
    size=$(du -m "$log_file" | cut -f1)
    
    if [ "$size" -gt "$MAX_SIZE_MB" ]; then
        timestamp=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
        gzip -c "$log_file" > "$BACKUP_DIR/$(basename $log_file .log)_${timestamp}.log.gz"
        > "$log_file"  # truncate, don't delete
        echo "Rotated: $log_file (${size}MB)"
    fi
done

# Eliminar backups viejos
find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name '*.log.gz' -mtime +"$MAX_AGE_DAYS" -delete
echo "Cleanup done: removed backups older than ${MAX_AGE_DAYS} days"

# Recargar servicio para nuevos logs
systemctl reload myapp.service 2>/dev/null
```

```bash
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh
# Cron diario a las 2am
echo '0 2 * * * root /usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh >> /var/log/log-rotation.log 2>&1' > /etc/cron.d/log-rotation
```
What's consuming the most CPU and memory right now?

<tool_call>{"name": "exec_command", "arguments": {"command": "echo '=== TOP CPU ===' && ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -10 && echo '=== TOP MEMORY ===' && ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -10 && echo '=== SYSTEM OVERVIEW ===' && free -h && uptime", "timeout": 10}}</tool_call>
Setup fail2ban to protect SSH

```bash
# Instalar
sudo apt install fail2ban

# Configuración personalizada (no tocar jail.conf, usar jail.local)
sudo tee /etc/fail2ban/jail.local << 'EOF'
[DEFAULT]
bantime = 3600
findtime = 600
maxretry = 3
ignoreip = 127.0.0.1/8 192.168.0.0/16

[sshd]
enabled = true
port = ssh
filter = sshd
logpath = /var/log/auth.log
maxretry = 3
bantime = 86400
EOF

sudo systemctl enable fail2ban
sudo systemctl start fail2ban

# Verificar
fail2ban-client status sshd
fail2ban-client status  # ver todas las jails
```
Deploy my app with zero-downtime using Docker

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# zero-downtime-deploy.sh
IMAGE="myapp:latest"
CONTAINER_NAME="myapp"

echo "Pulling new image..."
docker pull $IMAGE

echo "Starting new container..."
docker run -d \
  --name ${CONTAINER_NAME}_new \
  --env-file .env \
  -p 127.0.0.1:8001:8000 \
  $IMAGE

# Health check
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    if curl -sf http://localhost:8001/health; then
        echo "New container healthy!"
        break
    fi
    sleep 2
done

# Switch traffic (update nginx upstream)
nginx -s reload

# Stop old container
docker stop $CONTAINER_NAME
docker rm $CONTAINER_NAME
docker rename ${CONTAINER_NAME}_new $CONTAINER_NAME

echo "✅ Zero-downtime deploy complete"
```
